role_summary: Highlander Folk School music teacher and folk musician; co-taught the freedom-song corpus to SNCC and SCLC organizers from 1960 onward with her husband Guy Carawan.
ai_reading: Candie Anderson Carawan shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 11 (catalog slug 'candie-carawan-and-guy-hughes-carawan'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Candie Anderson Carawan is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 11 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Candie Anderson Carawan; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. Candie Anderson Carawan's Civil Rights History Project oral history is cross-referenced against the Library of Congress published transcript.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 11
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.alt: Highlander Folk School near Monteagle, Tennessee, 1930s, with Myles Horton
gallery.caption: The Highlander Folk School near Monteagle, Tennessee, where Carawan worked as a cultural educator and singer and helped teach the freedom-song corpus.
